'''Paul Wood''' (born 10 October 1981) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward in the 2000s and 2010s. He played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and [[Featherstone Rovers]] and the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]]. At international level, he made a non-Test appearance for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] in 2003, and was capped twice by [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2005. He is the Former head-coach of Swinton Lions, 30/03/2026

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Wood was signed by [[Warrington Wolves]] from Ince St Williams in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |title=Full blooded derby for Wood |url=https://www.warrington-worldwide.co.uk/2009/11/24/full-blooded-derby-for-wood/ |website=Warrington Worldwide |access-date=30 July 2023 |date=24 November 2009}}</ref> He made his first team debut in September 2000 against [[Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Prop forward Paul Wood given Warrington Wolves testimonial |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/4432799.prop-forward-paul-wood-given-warrington-wolves-testimonial/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=30 July 2023 |date=11 June 2009}}</ref>

Wood played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6–30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live |df=dmy }}</ref>
Wood also played in the 2012 Challenge Cup victory also against Leeds. Wood has continued to feature for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] during 2011 but his season ended early due to an injury in the lower back. During his long career at Warrington he has gained a reputation for the hard yards going forward, work rate & offloading.

Wood played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Wood played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against Leeds at [[Old Trafford]]<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|accessdate=20 November 2019}}</ref> During the game Wood ruptured his right testicle after getting kneed in the groin, one minute into the second half.<ref name="Wood loses testicle in Grand Final">{{cite web|url=http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/news/wood-loses-testicle-grand-final-125018484.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121010004845/http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/news/wood-loses-testicle-grand-final-125018484.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=10 October 2012|title=Wood loses testicle in Grand Final|publisher=uk.eurosport.yahoo.com|date=1 October 2012|accessdate=1 January 2013}}</ref> Wood continued to play, made a number of tackles and even conducted media interviews in the dressing room after the match without mentioning it. It became public knowledge only when he tweeted a couple of hours later that he was heading for hospital for surgery and he confirmed early on Sunday morning that his right testicle had been removed.<ref name="Warrington Wolves: Paul Wood has testicle removed">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19863795|title=Warrington Wolves: Paul Wood has testicle removed|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=7 October 2012|access-date=1 January 2013}}</ref>
Wood played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |accessdate=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archivedate=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://archive.today/20131006111732/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |accessdate=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Wood joined [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fresh challenge sought by Wood offered by Featherstone |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/fresh-challenge-sought-by-wood-offered-by-featherstone-1827047 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=23 April 2024 |date=8 January 2015}}</ref> He retired from playing at the end of the season and was hired as a coach at [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Former Warrington forward Paul Wood joins Leigh as a coach |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10058931/former-warrington-forward-paul-wood-joins-leigh-as-a-coach |website=Sky Sports |access-date=23 April 2024 |date=7 November 2015}}</ref> He briefly came out of retirement in 2018 to play for [[Swinton Lions]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Paul Wood joins Swinton |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/paul-wood-joins-swinton |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=23 April 2024 |date=28 July 2018}}</ref>

===Representative honours===
In 2003, Wood was selected in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad for the upcoming [[Ashes (rugby league)|Ashes]] series.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wood named in GB squad |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/3207988.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=22 April 2024 |date=20 October 2003}}</ref> He made his first and only appearance for the team in a non-Test game against a [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand 'A']] team. He left the game early due to a shoulder injury, which ruled him out of the Test series against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wood injury mars British success |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2003/oct/30/rugbyleague.andywilson |website=The Guardian |access-date=22 April 2024 |date=30 October 2003}}</ref> Wood received another Great Britain call-up for the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Tri-Nations]] as a replacement for the injured [[James Graham (rugby league)|James Graham]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Laybourn |first1=Ian |title=Wood's fine season earns spot in Tri-Nations squad |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/wood-s-fine-season-earns-spot-in-trinations-squad-421287.html |website=The Independent |access-date=22 April 2024 |date=23 October 2006}}</ref> but made no appearance during the tournament.

He was capped twice by [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2005, making his debut in a 22–12 win against [[France national rugby league team|France]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=France serve notice of intent, but England redress balance |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/oct/24/rugbyleague.englandrugbyleagueteam |website=The Guardian |access-date=22 April 2024 |date=24 October 2005}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
===Swinton Lions===
On 27 Oct 2024 it was reported that he had taken the role of head-coach for [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[RFL League 1]] for the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=27 Oct 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=Former Warrington Wolves star lands shock League 1 coaching role|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-warrington-wolves-star-lands-shock-league-1-coaching-role}}</ref>

On 30 March 2026 it was reported that he had stepped down, with assistant coach [[Anthony Murray (rugby league, born 1977)|Anthony Murray]] taking over<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Mar 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Paul Wood resigns as Swinton Lions head coach with replacement named|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/paul-wood-resigns-swinton-30-03-2026}}</ref>
